MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Rob W. Lawton of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing for defendant / moving party Timec Company, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Victor Gomez

RULING:
DEFENDANT TIMEC COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Timec Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. However even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff's declaration would create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
